LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER RCS-BROTHERHOOD WAY, LLC'S PETITION TO STAY ARBITRATION IS DENIED. THE ARBITRATOR, NOT THIS COURT, WILL DETERMINE WHETHER MBW-BROTHERHOOD WAY, LLC HAS STANDING TO SEEK ARBITRATION UNDER THE OPERATING AGREEMENT. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE MINI-MINUTES AND/OR ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Stay Arbitration
Off Calendar Oct-27-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Petitioner RCS-Brotherhood Way, LLC's petition to stay arbitration is denied. Respondent MBW-Brotherhood Way, LLC demanded arbitration pursuant to paragraph 14.18 of the Operating Agreement of RCS-Brotherhood Way, LLC. Paragraph.14.2 of the Operating Agreement provides that "[t]his agreement and any other agreements contemplated by this Agreement'shall be construed and enforced in accordance with and governed by the laws of the state of Colorado to the maximum extent permitted by law." (emphasis added.) Petitioner's argument that the procedural provisions of the California Arbitration Act apply and the court should stay the arbitration pursuant to CCP 1281.2(c) lacks merit. Parties may expressly designate that arbitration move forward under non-California procedural rules. (See Valencia v. Smyth (2010) 185 Cal.App.4th 153, 174.) By agreeing to use Colorado laws "to the maximum extent permitted by law," the parties to the Operating Agreement chose both Colorado procedural law and substantive law. Petitioner does not identify a Colorado stay provision analogous to CCP 1281.2(c) and thus there is no legal basis to grant the stay requested by petitioner. (See Mastick v. TD Ameritrade, Inc. (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1258, 1266.) The arbitrator, not this court, will determine whether MBW-Brotherhood Way, LLC has standing to seek arbitration under the Operating Agreement. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
